titleStmt.title: Transcription and Translation of DCA DCD Misc. Ch. 623

respStmt.resp: Exported from Models of Authority data
respStmt.name: tei_exporter

availability.p: Status unknown. Exported for research and migration workflows.

revisionDesc.change: transcription moa=41 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-13 14:14:00.663353+00 | translation moa=42 status=reviewed modified=2023-06-13 14:14:00.620743+00

div.p:
Alex’ . dei gratia Rex Scott’ . Omnibus probis hominibus Tocius terre sue Clericis et Laicis  Salutem .
Sciant presentes et futuri nos concessisse et hac Carta nostra confirmasse Donationem illam quam
Dauid de Quikeswude fecit deo et sancto Cuthberto et sancte Ebbe . et Monachis Dunolmensibus apud Col
dingham’ . seruientibus et in perpetuum seruituris de tota terra sua quam habuit in uilla de Alde
cambus et extra . pro Excambio Sexaginta acrarum in territorio de Coldingham’ . et uno Tofto et Crofto
que Waldeuus pethun tenuit et decem Acris in Cultura de Floris . et uno Mesuagio iuxta Fabricam
Benedicti Fabri . Quare volumus . vt predicti Monachi prenominatam terram habeant et teneant . ita
libere et quiete  sicut Carta predicti Dauid et Confirmatio Thome filij eiusdem dauid . inde facte predictis
Monachis testantur . saluo seruicio nostro . Testibus . Henrico . de Balliol’ . Camerario . Patricio . filio . Comitis Patricii .
Radulfo . Capellano . Thoma . de Striuelin . Clerico . Henrico . de Striuelin . filio . Comitis . dauid . Johanne de Maccuswel’ . Waltero .
Cumin’ . Apud Seleschr’ . ix . die Decembris . Anno Regni domini Regis . decimo.

Alexander, by the grace of God king of Scots, to all worthy men of his whole land, clerk and lay, greeting: Let present and future know that we have granted and by this our charter to have made firm the donation which David of Quixwood made to God and to Saint Cuthbert and to Saint Æbbe, and to the monks of Durham at Coldingham, to those serving now and in perpetuity to those who shall serve in future, of his whole land which he had within and outwith the toun of Aldcambus, for the exchange of sixty acres in the territory of Coldingham, and one toft and croft which Waldef Pethun held and ten acres of arable land at Fleurs, and one messuage near the smiddy of Benedict the Smith. Wherefore we will, that the aforesaid monks aforementioned have and hold the land, as freely and quietly, as the charter of the aforesaid David and the confirmation of Thomas the son of the same David, made thereupon for the aforesaid monks declares, saving our service. As witnesses, Henry de Balliol, Chamberlain, Patrick son of Earl Patrick, Ralph the Chaplain, Thomas of Stirling the Clerk, Henry of Stirling, son of Earl David, John of Maxwell, Walter Comyn. At Selkirk, on the 9th day of December, in the Year of the Reign of the lord King, the Tenth.
